title: OpenClaw exec allowlist skipped argPattern on Linux/macOS, permitting disallowed argv
description: OpenClaw's exec allowlist supports optional argPattern entries that restrict which argv shapes are accepted for an allowlisted executable. In matchAllowlist() the argPattern check is gated on the platform: useArgPattern is only true when the effective platform string starts with 'win'. On Linux and macOS gateways (useArgPattern === false) the loop returns the first allowlist entry whose executable path matches and hits 'if (!useArgPattern) { return entry; }', skipping the configured argPattern argv check entirely. An operator who configures an allowlist entry that appears to permit only a narrow argv shape therefore has that restriction silently ignored, so the agent can run other argv for the same executable without an approval prompt when tools.exec.security is set to 'allowlist'.
